solutions.solution: Probably true. The incentive principle is part of the Constitution as a statement that means that the political inhibitions referred to in the passage mean that deals, there is given inference appears likely to be true.

Definitely false. Although the last sentence may give an impression that the inference is not, you must also see the first sentence speaks about fact that divestment in public sector has already taken place.
Definitely false. The second sentence says that many people treat it as a synonym of privatisation. But the first sentence disputes this fact by stating that they have different meanings.
Definitely true. It is obvious.
Definitely false. Since the incentive principles are a part of the Constitution, they are at least as old as the Constitution.
Definitely false. See the second sentence ('But a chasm......').

Probably true. Both these persons were involved in the process of preparing the Constitution. It is likely to be false that they disapproved of something which was incorporated as an important part (directing principles) of the Constitution.
Data Inadequate. ICDS was started next year. Hence, ICDS was started in 1975. But was it May? We cannot be sure.

Probably true. This sentence says that there is now because new there is a backlog of 'political will' with enhanced hopes of improvement, it is very likely that, earlier, lack of political will was the reason for lack of improvement.
Definitely false. It is assumed that only 30 million people will come from among poor line. Actually many more are living below poverty line.
Definitely false. The passage has, in fact, proved that the JRY is short of funds.
Probably true. By the year 2000 A.D., inflation, monsoon etc. would have the effect on the level of Rs 45 and are likely to push it upwards.
Definitely true. See the fourth sentence ('This requirement will also build dependent on monsoons......etc.').
Definitely false. See explanation to Q 35.
Data Inadequate. There is no hint about the state of affairs before 1958.
Definitely false. The last sentence says that out of 1438 products, only 129 are under compulsory certification. Hence, it appears false that there are more items of mass consumption than others.
Definitely false. Books are not items of mass consumption, so the classification would be voluntary and not compulsory.

Probably false. The first sentence says the reforms are aimed at 'a big cross-section' of the economy. So, it is unlikely that it is to benefit only the rich. But it is not mentioned that the 'wide cross-section' is to be affected by the basis of 'income' so we cannot say that the inference is definitely false.
Definitely true. The passage says that after reforms there has been an introduction of capital adequacy norms. Introduction means that it has come for the first time.
Definitely false. The resource allocation was also inefficient. See the third sentence.

Definitely true. The last sentence talks of 'greater public confidence' which will 'ensure' safety......and 'bring about' transparency......and which will be 'relevant to resource mobilisation.' So it is seen that public confidence is not entirely relevant to resource mobilisation.
Data Inadequate. The passage neither supports nor denies that the reforms are aimed only at the financial sector.
Definitely false. The policy strives to achieve the objective 'downman' to the maxim 'spare the rod......child'.
Definitely false. 'Thirty fifty years hence' the attitude had not changed. It means that the attitude changed only after the 1940s.
Definitely false. Did it indicate that it was thought to be an intellectual aberration but this attitude has now increased in case-of-change.
Probably true. One of the likely reasons for the unhealthy growth of a child. But it is not definitely true because there can be other reasons too.

Probably false. The first line says that today we have a 'fast-changing world.' The inference looks likely to be false in the context of a fast-changing world, but there is no definite information in the passage about whether there is any change in attitude in any other area as well. Hence, we can't say that it is definitely false.
Probably true. The first sentence says that 'dominance by public sector' is 'a distinct feature of the Indian financial system'. The word distinct implies that such a thing is uncommon.

Probably true. The first sentence says that there is dominance of public sector. The second sentence says that this was motivated by socio-economic considerations. So, it must have been assumed that public sector would serve socio-economic purpose better than private sector.
Definitely true. (There are 'varying degrees of concessionality' in interest rates.)
